description: Southeast Asian Islamist militant group based in Indonesia, founded on 1 January 1993 by Abu Bakar Bashir and Abdullah Sungkar. Dedicated to establishing an Islamic state in Southeast Asia, it perpetrated the 2002 Bali bombings and other attacks. Designated as a terrorist group by the UN and multiple countries. Disbanded in 2024.

country_links.evidence_quote: On 17 May 2024, a Royal Malaysian Police station at Ulu Tiram, Johor was attacked by a man that was linked with Jemaah Islamiyah, killing two police officers and wounding one.
country_links.context: A man linked with Jemaah Islamiyah attacked a police station in Malaysia, killing two officers.
